<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The aggregate market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by non-affiliates of the registrant on June 30, 2010 was approximately $6,084,392.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The registrant had 84,294,938 shares of common stock and no shares of preferred stock outstanding as of April 14, 2011.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">DOCUMENTS INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The following documents are incorporated herein by reference in Part&#160;IV, Item 15:&#160;&#160;(i)&#160;Registration Statement on Form&#160;10SB, filed on January&#160;18, 2002, (ii)&#160;Current Report on Form&#160;8-K, filed on April&#160;7, 2005, (iii)&#160;Schedule 14C Definitive Information Statement, filed June 28, 2005, (iv) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed June 11, 2009, (v) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed August 26, 2009, (vi) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed September 18, 2009, (vii) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed January 22, 2010, (viii) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed April 6, 2010, (ix) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed June 21, 2010, and (x) Current Report on Form 8-K, filed July 21, 2010.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">ITEM 1.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;BUSINESS<a name="Item1_Business_123611"><!--EFPlaceholder--></a></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Corporate History</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We were incorporated in the State of Nevada on October&#160;12, 2000 under the name Cogen Systems,&#160;Inc.&#160; We changed our name to Snocone Systems,&#160;Inc. on December&#160;6, 2001.&#160; On April&#160;1, 2005, Snocone Systems,&#160;Inc. and its wholly-owned subsidiary, WYD Acquisition Corp., a California corporation (the &#8220;Merger Sub&#8221;), completed and closed an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Who&#8217;s Your Daddy,&#160;Inc. (&#8220;WYD&#8221;), an unrelated, privately held California corporation, whereby the Merger Sub merged with and into WYD.&#160; After the merger, the separate existence of the Merger Sub ceased and, as such, WYD continued its corporate existence as a direct, wholly-owned subsidiary of Snocone Systems,&#160;Inc. under the laws of the State of California.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April&#160;13, 2005, the shareholders of Snocone Systems,&#160;Inc. voted to change our name to Who&#8217;s Your Daddy,&#160;Inc. and effective June 1, 2010, the shareholders of Who&#8217;s Your Daddy, Inc. voted to change our name to FITT Highway Products, Inc.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Business Development</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">After developing an energy drink with several flavors, we changed our primary business to the manufacture (on an outsource basis), sale and distribution of these beverages centered on the trademark-protected brands, Who&#8217;s Your Daddy&#174; and The King of Energy&#174;&#160;which we began selling in 2005.&#160;&#160;We originally marketed our energy drinks through retail outlets mainly to a demographic of customers in their late teens through mid-thirties who were seeking alternatives to bad tasting energy drinks, coffee and other stimulants and were attracted to our products because of their energy boosting capabilities, pleasant taste, and also because of our edgy and provocative tradenames.&#160;&#160;Given the crowded energy drink market and high cost of acquiring shelf space, in late 2008, we developed and began test-marketing a two-ounce Sports Energy Shot in limited retail markets to determine the best marketing strategy and demographic for this type of product.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Based on what we learned, during the second quarter of 2009, we determined we would modify our product and expand our target demographic in order to be competitive in the energy product marketplace.&#160;&#160;During the last three quarters of 2009 and most of the 2010 year, the Company temporarily suspended sales activity to focus on modifying our Sports Energy Shot and changing its name to &#8220;F.I.T.T. Energy With Resveratrol&#8221; (the &#8220;FITT Energy Shot&#8221;).&#160;&#160;The FITT Energy Shot is essentially the same formula as our Sports Energy Shot, but also contains additional ingredients which various scientific studies describe as having certain possible health and fitness benefits.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As a result of our decision to concentrate on our new FITT Energy Shot product, we decided in 2009 to no longer use the Who&#8217;s Your Daddy&#174; and The King of Energy&#174; tradenames, and effective January 19, 2010, we entered into a settlement agreement with Fish&#160;&amp; Richardson, P.C. (&#8220;Fish&#8221;), a previous provider of legal services to us, wherein we agreed to transfer all right, title and interest in these tradenames to Fish, and Fish agreed to acknowledge a full satisfaction of any debt owed Fish by the Company.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Effective August 12, 2010, the we entered into an Operating Agreement with F.I.T.T. Energy Products, Inc. (&#8220;FITT&#8221;), a private Nevada corporation controlled by certain of the Company&#8217;s&#160;&#160;investors and management, but not deemed under common control.&#160;&#160;Under the terms of&#160;the Operating Agreement, FITT performs certain operating services for the Company, including among other things, selling, marketing, producing and distributing the FITT Energy Shot, and will pay all costs and expenses involved with performing these services.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">FITT began test marketing the FITT Energy Shot using a new marketing strategy pointing to customers in their twenties, thirties, and forties who are interested in fitness and health as well as gaining an energy boost.&#160;&#160;The test marketing began with an internet-based model using email blasts, key word searches and Direct Response Television (&#8220;DRTV&#8221;) to attract customers, so that we could test call center, fulfillment and other sales and administrative processes.&#160;&#160;We also believed that this test marketing strategy would allow us to generate brand awareness that could then be leveraged in an all-important roll-out of our product to the retail market.&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">1</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Description of Business</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Our Company&#8217;s business is the manufacture (on an outsource basis), distribution and sale of energy drinks which, as noted in Operations below, is currently being performed by FITT.&#160;&#160;The primary outlet for our product is expected to be the retail market, with additional sales coming from our website and DRTV presence.&#160;&#160;Marketing support and brand recognition will be primarily done through television.&#160;&#160;Our revenues and cash flow is presently limited to the receipt of royalties from FITT of $0.05 per bottle sold of the FITT Energy Shot.&#160;&#160;As such, we are now dependent upon the success of FITT&#8217;s operations.&#160;&#160;Until we can mitigate the Company&#8217;s debt, we will be unable to operate in the normal course of business and seek capital to develop new products.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Products</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Energy Shots</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Our energy shots were designed in collaboration with Dr. Rand Scott, a Board Certified Anesthesiologist and Pain Management Specialist.&#160;&#160;Dr. Scott is a graduate of Penn State University and a former player and physician for its football team. He is on the board of PriCara Pharmaceutical, a Johnson &amp; Johnson Company and is currently a consultant to Scisco Group, Inc. as well as an expert in herbal products.&#160;&#160;Dr. Scott is also a member of the Speakers Board for Pfizer Pharmaceutical and speaks across the United States on pain management.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">FITT Energy Shot</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The FITT Energy Shot, which is a modification of our Sports Energy Shot, contains some of the most exciting&#160;supplements of this generation.&#160;These&#160;ingredients have been selected to not only provide energy, but to also enhance mental focus, muscle strength and endurance, and promote cardiovascular health.&#160; The FITT Energy Shot features Resveratrol.&#160; A substance found naturally in grapes, Resveratrol may cause the body to act as if it is already on a diet, and change the distribution of fat tissue in the body. In fact, Resveratrol has the scientific world fascinated by its potential to affect age related decline.&#160; Our FITT Energy Shot also contains L-Arginine, an amino acid in dairy, brown rice and nuts which is essential for optimum growth, and regulation of protein metabolism.&#160;&#160;L-Arginine can make blood vessels wider, as opposed to the narrowing effect of caffeine. Further, L-Arginine may benefit in the treatment of sports related injuries, as well in building lean muscle and burning fat, since it facilitates the natural release of growth hormone (HGH) and is a building block for creatine. Additionally, the drink features L-Arginine alphaketoglutarate (AKG).&#160; Arginine AKG has been shown in a University study to help build additional strength when used during training.&#160; Beyond this, the FITT Energy Shot features antioxidant Green Tea extract, and Chromium.&#160; These ingredients have good safety profiles and have support as weight-loss aides.&#160; More than just a caffeine drink, our FITT Energy Shot adds natural energy boosters including Taurine &amp; Guarana, as well as essential Vitamins B3, B5, B6, and B12.&#160;To optimize workouts, the FITT Energy Shot has a touch of Fructose, an easily absorbed fuel for the body and brain.&#160; All this is built on a base of healthy pomegranate and orange.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Sports Energy Shot</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Sports Energy Shot, a concentrated two-ounce energy drink, designed to provide a zero calorie, sugar free, rapid and lasting energy boost which enhances muscle strength and endurance. The Sports Energy Shot contains many of the same ingredients as the FITT Energy Shot including L-Arginine and the formulation used in the Sports Energy Shot was the basis for the FITT Energy Shot&#8217;s formulation.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Canned Energy Drink Products</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We previously distributed our canned energy drinks in two flavors, Cranberry-Pineapple and Green Tea, with a Regular and Sugar-Free version of each.&#160;&#160;Shipments began in 2005 with the Cranberry-Pineapple flavor.&#160;&#160;During 2009, we stopped selling the canned energy drinks to focus on our energy shots, but we may elect to resume sales of these products in the future, under the FITT brand, in those situations where marketing, shipping, and product placement costs are minimal.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Operations</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Since the Company completed its merger with Snocone Systems, Inc., we have been unable to generate income and have become burdened with substantial debt.&#160;&#160;As of December 31, 2010, we have less than $3,000 in assets (zero in cash) and in excess of $4.8 million in debt, which includes approximately $1.8 million for an accrued arbitration award for a lawsuit against the Company.&#160;&#160;As a result, we have been unable to attract necessary investment dollars <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">to our Company to produce our product and implement our marketing strategy.&#160;&#160;In addition, third parties performing marketing, production and fulfillment services have been unwilling to enter into agreements directly with us.&#160;&#160;These factors raise substantial doubt about the Company&#8217;s ability to continue as a going concern unless it can substantially mitigate its debt and raise capital.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">2</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In order to conduct operations, we have been forced to pursue a partner to produce the FITT Energy Shot and to implement the marketing plan.&#160;&#160; Under the terms of&#160;the Operating Agreement with FITT, they will perform certain operating services for the Company, including among other things, selling, marketing, producing and distributing the FITT Energy Shot, and will pay all costs and expenses involved with performing these services.&#160;&#160;For its part, FITT will pay the Company a royalty of $0.05 for each bottle sold of the FITT Energy Shot.&#160;&#160;In connection with the Operating Agreement, the Company agreed to issue FITT 5,000,000 of its common shares, which were fully vested as of the date of issuance.&#160;&#160;While FITT has indicated it will use the shares as an inducement to attract investment dollars from interested investors, it is not required to do so.&#160;&#160;FITT will process and record in its books all sales, costs and operating expenses connected with its performance of services in connection with the Operating Agreement, and all cash, inventory and other assets resulting from either the invested dollars or from FITT&#8217;s operations will be the property of FITT.&#160;&#160;While FITT will enjoy the benefit of any profits earned through its performance of the operating services, it will also bear the responsibility of any losses as well as raising capital.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">FITT will be performing the majority of the operating functions for the Company and compensate us with a royalty payment described above.&#160;&#160;If we are successful with plans to mitigate our debt, we expect the Company will be able to attract investment, enter agreements with service providers, develop new products, and manage its operating functions.&#160;&#160;Management cannot be certain that royalty funds received from FITT, after the payment of amounts owed to FITT and an IRS tax levy, will be sufficient to pay our basic operating expenses, let alone mitigate debt in any substantial way.&#160;&#160;Management continues to actively seek capital through various sources, but given the present economic environment and the Company&#8217;s current financial condition, management may not be able to attract any capital without first significantly mitigating its debt.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Marketing</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In July 2010, FITT entered into an agreement with Euro RSCG Edge (&#8220;Euro&#8221;) whereby Euro would perform a variety of marketing services for FITT including creating a 60 second television commercial (the &#8220;FITT Commercial&#8221;) about the FITT Energy Shot.&#160;&#160;By changing the tag line at the end of the FITT Commercial, it can be easily customized for use in specific markets for specific offers.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Euro is a member of Euro RSCG Worldwide, which according to 2009 Advertising Age Global Marketers Report, is the largest global agency as measured by total number of global accounts.&#160;&#160;Euro agreed to market the FITT Energy Shot because they believe the product can be a big hit since it is significantly different than any other product in the rapidly growing energy shot category&#160;and is a healthy alternative.&#160; They are also impressed by the team of doctors, athletes, product producers, and call and fulfillment centers supporting the product.&#160;&#160;FITT intends to use the FITT Commercial to support multiple marketing activities including retail, DRTV and online programs.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2010, FITT began test marketing the FITT Energy Shot through DRTV and internet email campaigns as a precursor to a roll-out into the retail market.&#160;&#160;It was anticipated that test marketing would allow for an evaluation of the selling, call center and fulfillment processes for effectiveness and efficiency while determining whether DRTV and email broadcasts could generate brand recognition and produce a reasonable level of sales.&#160;&#160;The test marketing was also designed to allow for an evaluation of customers&#8217; responses to a multitude of product offerings including continuity programs, BOGO (buy one, get one free), reduced shipping costs and other types of offers.&#160;&#160;It was believed that, in addition to providing important information about core customers and markets, this approach would allow for the building of brand recognition by reaching a far greater number of customers at significantly reduced costs for marketing, shipping, and product placement than would have been experienced using traditional retail distribution methods.&#160;&#160;Investing marketing dollars in these ways, particularly through television, allows for millions of households to be reached in a short time while targeting specific demographics.&#160;&#160;All of this would prepare the brand for entry into the retail marketplace where it is anticipated that a more consistent level of sales, earnings and cash flow would be created.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Cash required to implement our marketing plan is significant.&#160;&#160;Investors are requiring that investment dollars be used within FITT for FITT Energy Shot operations in order to protect their investments from prior creditor claims of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">the Company.&#160;&#160;Since investors have typically been offered shares of our common stock as an additional incentive to invest, they also asked that we pursue additional funding to be used to mitigate existing debt of the Company at a maximum of 10 to 15 cents per dollar of debt.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">3</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Marketing Plan &#8211; Retail</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The retail market space for our product includes grocery chains, drug stores, convenience stores and health and fitness centers to name a few.&#160;&#160;We believe sales into the retail market will provide the most stable method for marketing the FITT Energy Shot and other products, but we also understand the retail market requires brand recognition and marketing support.&#160;&#160;Euro advised management that the FITT Commercial would be one of the most important features in helping to drive traffic to the stores, providing support for retailers and distributors.&#160;&#160;The tag line at the end of the commercial can be easily customized to indicate in exactly which outlets the product would be available.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During the last quarter of 2010, a major sports franchise expressed an interest in the FITT Energy Shot after viewing the FITT Commercial and reading about the product on FITT website.&#160;&#160;This sports franchise has relationships with the types of market outlets we are pursuing.&#160;&#160;FITT is currently in discussions with this organization to establish a working relationship which would allow the leveraging of their sports brand to provide exposure for the FITT Energy Shot in the market outlets they serve.&#160;&#160;We are hopeful that FITT will be able to negotiate and reach an agreement to launch a roll-out of the FITT Energy Shot in the market served by this sports franchise.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In April 2010, we announced an agreement integrating Warren Moon, NFL Hall of Fame quarterback, into an advertising campaign for the FITT Energy Shot featured in the new <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-STYLE: italic">24 Hour Fitness Digital Magazine</font> and the promotion of the FITT Energy Shot product beginning in May 2010.&#160;&#160;24 Hour Fitness is an innovative leader in the health and fitness industry and serves more than 3 million members in more than 425 clubs.&#160;&#160;We believe that strategic partnerships with Health and Fitness Clubs will allow us to reach potential customers interested in our product and the benefits they believe can be achieved in connection with their workouts.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">It is FITT&#8217;s intention to work with strategic relationships to develop joint marketing opportunities with distributors who deliver multiple products on a daily basis to retail outlets across the country.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Marketing Plan &#8211; DRTV</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">For many companies and products, DRTV has been proven to be a very efficient method of establishing recognition of a brand and product attributes while also generating sales and Euro has specialized in DRTV since 1998 with its very impressive talent team.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The driving force behind Euro&#8217;s success, and ultimately the success of the FITT Energy Shot through DRTV, is how they financially motivate their media buyers, who are 100% commission-driven. They earn a fixed percent of the agency&#8217;s commission if the buys they make on behalf of the client are executed in accordance with an agreed-upon strategic plan.&#160; They earn double that percentage on each show for which their monthly performance is in the top half of all buyers.&#160; Simply put, the better they perform in terms of results for their clients, the more they earn, and the greater the revenue from the FITT Energy Shot.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During the fourth quarter of 2010, FITT began test marketing the FITT Energy Shot on multiple television stations across the U.S.&#160;&#160;FITT has been evaluating the results of these tests to determine, among other things, which product offering will best attract a long-term customer demographic we are interested in.&#160;&#160;The test marketing results are also expected to provide information that will be important in the planning for a retail roll-out.&#160;&#160;The FITT Commercial, which can be seen at <font style="DISPLAY: inline; TEXT-DECORATION: underline">www.throwafitt.com</font>, is capable of generating sales by having a customer call an 800 number and ordering product through a call center.&#160;&#160;The tag line at the end of the commercial can be easily customized for different customer offers in order to help determine which offers best work with various consumer demographics.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Marketing Plan - Internet</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We began the internet marketing plan by test broadcasting emails to customers selected from a total of 88 million leads from two affiliates with which the Company has agreements.&#160;&#160;The test email broadcasts directed potential customers to a website that educated them about the FITT Energy Shot and included an endorsement by Warren Moon and three medical experts who are also investors in the Company.&#160;&#160;In addition to broadcasting email messages to a select group of leads, a test of a key word search program was begun.&#160;&#160;FITT is currently working with Euro to <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">review the internet portion of the marketing program to help refine and target this approach in an appropriate manner including developing social media and joint venture revenue sharing opportunities.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">4</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">It is expected that, because of the association with Warren Moon and his connections with high-profile athletes, there will be an ability to bring nationally recognizable sport figures to assist with the implementation of this aspect of the marketing plan and we are confident these relationships will help to enhance the image of the FITT Energy Shot and strengthen the product&#8217;s brand awareness.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Production and Distribution</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Under the FITT Operating Agreement, they will be responsible for the production and distribution of the FITT Energy Shot.&#160;&#160;The FITT Energy Shot is produced at Wellington Foods Incorporated, a contract manufacturer of liquid and powder nutritional supplements since 1974.&#160;&#160;In addition to its manufacturing facilities, Wellington has the in-house capabilities to develop products from concept for flavoring ingredient content to production, or to take an existing formula and extend the product line with new flavors or innovative ingredients.&#160;&#160;Dr. Rand Scott, one of our medical experts and a shareholder, provided the ingredients for our new energy shot formula and Wellington provided the final flavoring and formulation.&#160;&#160;Wellington owns the formula for the FITT Energy Shot, but there is no barrier to its recreation and there are numerous manufacturers within the U.S. capable of manufacturing the product.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The principal raw materials used to manufacture the energy shot are plastic bottles, nutritional supplements, flavoring agents, and concentrates as well as other ingredients from independent suppliers.&#160;&#160;These raw materials are readily available from any number of sources in the United States.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The FITT Energy Shot will initially be shipped to customers from the Laguna Hills, California facility of RTM Berlin Holdings, LLC, one of the nation&#8217;s leading fulfillment resources.&#160;&#160;Ultimately, it is anticipated that the product will be shipped from RTM&#8217;s 350,000 square foot facility in Neenah, Wisconsin.&#160;&#160;RTM serves clients in all areas of b-to-b and b-to-c marketing, including DRTV, catalogs, online shopping, continuity programs, literature fulfillment and CRM services. The customer service representatives at RTM are rigorously trained to understand every detail of our product/service, and strive to provide the highest possible levels of service. Their customer service includes inbound call processing, outbound sales projects, email customer service, product / technical information, promotion inquiry and order and shipping status inquiries. Because of RTM&#8217;s huge annual shipping volume, they are in a position to negotiate highly favorable shipping rates which are significantly lower than any single customer could negotiate. They are also skilled managers in providing our customers with expeditious, yet money saving ways of completing the final leg of distribution: shipping to the individual consumer or in bulk to a retail store or retail distribution point.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Industry</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Energy drinks, including two-ounce shots and canned drinks, are beverages with legal stimulants, vitamins, and minerals that give users a lift of energy.&#160;&#160;Common ingredients are caffeine, taurine, ginseng, sugars, and various amounts of vitamins and minerals.&#160;&#160;The products are consumed by individuals who are explicitly looking for the extra boost in energy.&#160;&#160;Energy shots, in particular, are meant for people who want a jolt of caffeine without having to drink a big cup of coffee or one of the 16-ounce energy drinks that have become ubiquitous. They go down fast, more like medicine than a beverage. That is part of the appeal to their most devoted consumers: students cramming for exams or partying into the night, construction workers looking for a lift, drivers trying to stay awake, fitness enthusiasts, the &#8220;on-the-go&#8221; average person, and those seeking an alternative to coffee. Tired, stressed-out college students and workers have embraced energy shots, which promise a quick, convenient boost with fewer calories and less sugar than full-size energy drinks.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Sales of the 2-to-3 ounce shots soared to $544 million in 2008, which is double the amount of the previous year, according to Information Resources Inc. (&#8220;IRI&#8221;), a Chicago-based market research firm. In fact, energy shots are the fastest-growing segment of the $4.6 billion energy drink market, according to the market research firm Mintel International Group Ltd.&#160;&#160;Living Essentials pioneered energy shots in 2004 with 5-Hour Energy, which still holds more than 75% of the market, says IRI. . Living Essentials has spent heavily on advertising to build the market and hold its position against newcomers. It has been reported that the company expects to spend $60 million this year on television advertising for 5-Hour Energy. Industry heavyweights such as Red Bull, Monster Energy, and Coca-Cola have since introduced their own energy shots. Sales of the energy shots are rising even as sales of traditional energy <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">drinks like Red Bull have flattened out. Based on sales data collected by IRI it is estimated that energy shot sales would be about $700 million in 2009, not counting sales of non-reporting entities like Wal-Mart Stores.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">5</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Competition</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The energy drink industry is intensely competitive and significantly affected by new product introductions and other market activities of industry participants.&#160; The principal areas of competition are pricing, packaging, development of new products and flavors, and marketing campaigns.&#160; The FITT Energy Shot product competes with a number of other energy shots produced by a relatively few number of manufacturers, most of which have substantially greater financial, marketing and distribution resources than we do.&#160; The principal competitors include 5 Hour Energy&#174;, Red Bull&#174;, and Monster Energy&#174; among many others.&#160;&#160;Management believes the FITT Energy Shot&#8217;s unique healthy formula and affiliation with qualified medical expert as well as renowned athletes will give the company a vastly different entry into a rapidly expanding market.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Intellectual Property</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We previously manufactured, promoted and sold our The King of Energy&#174; energy drinks under our trademark-protected brand, Who&#8217;s Your Daddy&#174;.&#160;&#160;In the third quarter of 2009, we decided to no longer use the Who&#8217;s Your Daddy&#174; and The King of Energy&#174; tradenames, and to investigate the development of new tradenames.&#160;&#160;Additionally, in October 2009, we were served with an action by Fish &amp; Richardson, P.C. (&#8220;Fish&#8221;) to foreclose on their security interest in the our tradenames.&#160;&#160;The security interest arose from a 2006 settlement agreement between the Company and Fish in connection with fees purportedly owed by us to Fish.&#160;&#160;Given the Company&#8217;s decision to no longer use its Tradenames, the Company began working on an agreement with Fish to affect an orderly transfer of the Tradenames to Fish and, effective January 19, 2010, we entered into a settlement agreement with Fish under which we agreed to transfer all right, title and interest in our tradenames to Fish, and Fish agreed to acknowledge a full satisfaction of all indebtedness owed them by the Company.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Company currently has no other intellectual property.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Employees</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As of the date of this Report, we employed two persons, both of whom are full-time.&#160; We retain independent contractors as needed.&#160; None of our employees are represented by labor unions and we believe that our employee relations are satisfactory.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">ITEM 1A.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RISK FACTORS<a name="Item1a_RiskFactors_123623"><!--EFPlaceholder--></a></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We are a smaller reporting company as defined by Rule&#160;12b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and are not required to provide the information under this item.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">ITEM 1B.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;UNRESOLVED STAFF COMMENTS<a name="Item1b_UnresolvedStaffComments_123624"><!--EFPlaceholder--></a></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We are a smaller reporting company as defined by Rule&#160;12b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and are not required to provide the information under this item.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">ITEM 2.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PROPERTIES<a name="Item2_Properties_123626"><!--EFPlaceholder--></a></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We do not own any real property.&#160; Our principal executive offices are located at 26381 Crown Valley Parkway, Suite&#160;230, Mission Viejo, CA 92691 where we are leasing approximately 900 square feet under a month-to-month agreement that commenced in June 2009.&#160; Monthly payments under the lease are currently $2,000.&#160; As we have out-sourced manufacturing and product fulfillment, including product storage, we consider our leased office space adequate for the operation of our business.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">ITEM 3.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;LEGAL PROCEEDINGS<a name="Item3_LegalProceedings_123628"><!--EFPlaceholder--></a></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Sacks Motor Sports Inc.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On July&#160;19, 2006, we received a Demand for Arbitration filed with the American Arbitration Association from Sacks Motor Sports Inc. (&#8220;Sacks&#8221;) seeking damages arising out of a sponsorship contract.&#160; On February&#160;13, 2007, the Arbitrator awarded Sacks $1,790,000.&#160; This amount was recorded as an expense in the quarter ending December&#160;31, 2006 and is fully reserved on the balance sheet.&#160; On August&#160;6, 2007, we filed a petition in U.S. District Court asking the judge to either: (1)&#160;order the arbitrator to reopen the arbitration and allow for discovery regarding what we believe to be significant new evidence to have the award vacated; or (2)&#160;to allow us to conduct such discovery in the U.S. District Court proceeding regarding what we believe to be significant new evidence to have the award vacated.&#160; On May&#160;27, 2008, the Court denied our petition and entered judgment in favor of Sacks for the principal sum of $1,790,000 together with post-award interest from February&#160;13, 2007.&#160; On July&#160;16, 2008, we entered into an Assignment of Claims Agreement (&#8220;Assignment Agreement&#8221;) with Anga M&#8217;Hak Publishing (&#8220;Anga M&#8217;Hak&#8221; ) and Edward Raabe, for the consideration of 150,000 shares of common stock and $100,000 in cash out of the proceeds of our proposed private placement.&#160; We believe Anga M&#8217;Hak has a claim to offset the approximately $1,500,000 of the Sacks judgment.&#160; As part of the Assignment Agreement, Anga M&#8217;Hak and Raabe agreed to execute affidavits detailing their entitlement to the above-referenced claims and monies.&#160; In addition, they agreed to appear for depositions and as witnesses in court and to otherwise fully cooperate in our pursuit of these claims.&#160; We believe their affidavits will indicate that Sacks perpetrated fraud by not having the authority to enter into the contract, which wrongfully created the judgment in favor of Sacks.</font></div>

<br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">6</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Effective March 30, 2010, we entered into a Settlement Agreement and Release (the &#8220;Sacks Settlement&#8221;) with Sacks and with Greg Sacks (&#8220;Greg&#8221;).&#160;&#160;Under the Sacks Settlement, the Company agreed to pay Sacks $100,000 on or before April 15, 2010 and to issue Sacks 1,000,000 shares of its common stock in the form of 10 certificates of 100,000 shares each (the &#8220;Sacks Shares&#8221;).&#160;&#160;The Sacks Settlement called for the Sacks Shares to be delivered to the Company&#8217;s law firm, Solomon Ward Seidenwurm &amp; Smith, LLP (&#8220;SWSS&#8221;) with an irrevocable instruction to mail to Sacks one stock certificate of 100,000 shares per month for 10 consecutive months commencing July 15, 2010. The Sacks Shares will become free-trading upon receipt of a legal opinion from the Company&#8217;s counsel.&#160;&#160;Sacks agreed that it will not directly or indirectly sell, transfer or assign more than 100,000 shares of the Sacks Shares during any thirty day period at any time.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Sacks Settlement provided that, once the Company made the $100,000 payment and delivered the Sacks Shares to SWSS, Sacks would irrevocably waive, release and surrender all rights relating to or arising from its May 28, 2008 judgment against us and would take all actions reasonably requested by us to cause the judgment to be permanently rendered of no force or effect including without limitation by stipulating to set aside and vacate the judgment and cause then entire litigation to be dismissed with prejudice.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">While the Company issued the Sacks Shares to Sacks on March 30, 2010, we were unable to raise the $100,000 payment due April 15, 2010, and therefore were could not perform our obligations under the Sacks Settlement including the delivery of the Sacks Shares to SWSS.&#160;&#160;We are still attempting to raise the $100,000 and, if successful, are hopeful that Sacks will honor the Sacks Settlement, although there is no certainty that Sacks will do so.&#160;&#160;We have recorded the Sacks Shares at their par value of $1,000 and included the amount in prepaid expenses in the Company&#8217;s December 31, 2010 Balance Sheet.&#160;&#160;In the event we are not able to complete the Sacks Settlement, the Sacks Shares will be cancelled.&#160;&#160;If the Sacks Settlement is completed, the Company will value the shares on the date of settlement for use in the calculation of gain or loss on extinguishment of accrued litigation.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt">Fish &amp; Richardson</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On or about May&#160;15, 2008, Fish&#160;&amp; Richardson, P.C. (&#8220;Fish&#8221;) filed an action against us in the Superior Court of California, County of San Diego, asserting claims for breach of a settlement agreement purportedly entered into in connection with fees allegedly owed by us to Fish for Fish&#8217;s providing of legal services on the Company&#8217;s behalf in the approximate amount of $255,000.&#160;&#160;The settlement agreement, dated September 27, 2006, also granted Fish a security interest in all of the Tradenames owned by the Company and all associated goodwill.&#160;&#160;In its response to the <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Fish action, the Company asserted that the settlement agreement was void and that Fish failed to act as reasonably careful attorneys in connection with their representation of us.&#160;&#160;Fish brought a motion for summary judgment which was heard on April&#160;17, 2009, and the motion was granted.&#160;&#160;On May 21, 2009, a judgment was entered against the Company for $273,835 plus interest of $74,817 through the date of the judgment.&#160;&#160;On September 10, 2009, Fish filed an action to foreclose on their security interest in the Company&#8217;s tradenames, which action was served on the Company&#8217;s registered agent on approximately October 19, 2009.&#160;&#160;Given the Company&#8217;s decision to no longer use its Tradenames, the Company began working on an agreement with Fish to affect an orderly transfer of the Tradenames to Fish and, effective January 19, 2010, we entered into a settlement agreement with Fish wherein the Company agreed to transfer all right, title and interest in its tradenames to Fish and Fish agreed to acknowledge a full satisfaction of its judgment and to dismiss the Federal Action with prejudice.&#160;&#160;The Fish Settlement Agreement was executed on January 21, 2010.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">

<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">H.G. Fenton</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On or about July 22, 2009, H.G. Fenton Property Company (&#8220;Fenton&#8221;) filed a complaint against the Company in the Superior Court of California, County of San Diego, alleging Breach of Lease at our former office in Carlsbad, California (the &#8220;Carlsbad Lease&#8221;.)&#160;&#160;The complaint claimed damages in the amount of $420,000.&#160;&#160;In its answer to the complaint, the Company contended that Fenton has failed to mitigate damages, Fenton&#8217;s damages are speculative, and Fenton made certain representations concerning a lease restructure that the Company relied on to its detriment.&#160;&#160;On March 26, 2010, the Company attended a Case Management Conference during which a tentative trial date was set for January 14, 2011.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Effective December 17, 2010, the Company and Fenton executed a Stipulation for Entry of Judgment and Conditional Dismissal.&#160;&#160;Under the Stipulation, the parties agreed to accept a judgment against the Company in the amount of $294,590.&#160;&#160;The parties also agreed that the Company would pay Fenton a Settlement Payment of $20,000 and 200,000 shares of our common stock, which shares would be free-trading and unrestricted, and Fenton agreed to accept the Settlement Payment in full satisfaction of the judgment amount.&#160;&#160;In December 2010, the Company paid $10,000 of the Settlement Payment through an advance from FITT.&#160;&#160;In January 2011, a shareholder and former officer of the Company transferred 200,000 of his free-trading shares to Fenton.&#160;&#160;The Company made the final payment of $10,000, also through an advance from FITT,&#160;in March&#160;2011 in full satisfaction of its obligations under the Stipulation.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Christopher Wicks/Defiance</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On May&#160;8, 2007, we were served with a summons and complaint in a lawsuit filed in the San Diego Superior Court by Christopher Wicks and Defiance U.S.A.,&#160;Inc. seeking judgment against us, Edon Moyal and Dan Fleyshman under a contract allegedly calling for the payment of $288,000 in cash plus stock in our subsidiary, Who&#8217;s Your Daddy,&#160;Inc., a California corporation, and a certain percentage of the revenues of that subsidiary.&#160; On February&#160;1, 2008, we entered into a Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release with the plaintiffs pursuant to which we agreed to pay to the plaintiffs the sum of $252,000 under a payment schedule detailed therein.&#160; As security for the settlement payment, defendants Fleyshman and Moyal together pledged 317,210 shares of common stock in the Company owned and held by them.&#160; We are currently in default of the Settlement Agreement.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt">Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April&#160;1, 2005, the Company received a complaint filed by Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy,&#160;Inc., a Florida corporation (&#8220;Daddy&#8221;), alleging that the Company was infringing on Daddy&#8217;s trademark, Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy&#174;, with respect to clothing.&#160;&#160;On April&#160;7, 2006, the Company entered into a settlement agreement with Daddy pursuant to which the Company was granted an exclusive license to use its marks on clothing in exchange for a royalty payment of 6% of gross sales for clothing products in the United States, excluding footwear.&#160;&#160;As part of the settlement, the Company also agreed to remit to Daddy 12% of the licensing revenues received from third parties who the Company granted sublicense to for use of the marks on clothing.&#160;&#160;The Company was unable to make any of the required payments under the settlement agreement.&#160;&#160;On March&#160;26, 2008, the Company, Dan Fleyshman and Edon Moyal each received a Notice of Levy from the United States District Court for the Southern District of California in the amount of $143,561 allegedly pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement with Daddy.&#160;&#160;The Company settled the debt on March&#160;4, 2009 for $125,000 of which $25,000 was paid through an advance by a shareholder.&#160;&#160;The remaining balance was to be repaid with bi-monthly payments of $10,000 beginning April&#160;30, 2009.&#160;&#160;The Company has not made any additional payments and is in default of the most recent settlement agreement.&#160;&#160;As such, Daddy may elect <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">to declare the recent settlement agreement null and void and resume its pursuit of the amount due under the original settlement agreement, but the Company has not yet been notified that Daddy has chosen to do so.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">8</font></div>

<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
