<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" width="100%" style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">
<tr>
<td valign="top" width="2%" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">9.</font></div>
</td>
<td valign="top" width="98%" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">Loss on Relocation of Office</font></div>
</td>
</tr></table>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In May 2009, the Company moved its office from Carlsbad, California to a smaller, less expensive office in Mission Viejo, California.&#160;&#160;Because our Carlsbad lease ran through March 2012 and was still in force, we calculated our future minimum rent expenditures, adjusted for an estimate of the landlord&#8217;s future cash receipts from sublease based on current market lease rates, and accordingly recorded a loss relating to this lease of $208,884 for the year ended December 31, 2009.&#160;&#160;Additionally in the same period, we recorded a loss on disposal of property and equipment related to the move of $27,896, bringing the total loss resulting from the relocation of our office to $236,780.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During the third quarter 2010 we re-evaluated the estimates used in our loss calculation, since the property had not yet been re-leased, and increased the estimated loss by $93,506.&#160;&#160;Effective December 17, 2010, the Company and the landlord, H.G. Fenton Property Company (&#8220;Fenton&#8221;) executed a Stipulation for Entry of Judgment and Conditional Dismissal.&#160;&#160;Under the Stipulation, the parties agreed to accept a judgment against the Company in the amount of $294,590.&#160;&#160;The parties also agreed that the Company would pay Fenton a Settlement Payment consisting of $20,000 cash, which FITT agreed to advance, and 200,000 shares of our common stock, which shares would be free-trading and unrestricted, which were valued at $20,000 on the effective date of the settlement, and Fenton agreed to accept the Settlement Payment in full satisfaction of the judgment amount.&#160;&#160;As of December 31, 2010, we had made arrangements for obtaining the cash and free-trading shares required for the Settlement Payment.&#160;&#160;The ensuing gain, combined with the charge during the third quarter, resulted in a net gain of $169,950 in 2010 relating to the office relocation as the settlement was realizable.&#160;&#160;The final portion of the Settlement Payment was made in March 2011.&#160;&#160;See Notes 11 and 12.&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">10.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Commitments and Contingencies</font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As discussed in Note 9, the Company vacated its office in Carlsbad, California and recorded an expense for our estimated net future rental expense under the non-cancellable operating lease with the Carlsbad landlord.&#160;&#160;The Company leases its current office space in Mission Viejo, California on a month-to-month basis and has no other non-cancellable operating leases.&#160; Rent expense under operating leases amounted to $20,700 and $55,340, respectively, for the years ended December&#160;31, 2010 and 2009, respectively.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><br>
<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">11.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Litigation</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Sacks Motor Sports Inc.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On July&#160;19, 2006, we received a Demand for Arbitration filed with the American Arbitration Association from Sacks Motor Sports Inc. (&#8220;Sacks&#8221;) seeking damages arising out of a sponsorship contract.&#160; On February&#160;13, 2007, the Arbitrator awarded Sacks $1,790,000.&#160; This amount was recorded as an expense in the quarter ending December&#160;31, 2006 and is fully reserved on the balance sheet.&#160; On August&#160;6, 2007, we filed a petition in U.S. District Court asking the judge to either: (1)&#160;order the arbitrator to reopen the arbitration and allow for discovery regarding what we believe to be significant new evidence to have the award vacated; or (2)&#160;to allow us to conduct such discovery in the U.S. District Court proceeding regarding what we believe to be significant new evidence to have the award vacated.&#160; On May&#160;27, 2008, the Court denied our petition and entered judgment in favor of Sacks for the principal sum of $1,790,000 together with post-award interest from February&#160;13, 2007.&#160; On July&#160;16, 2008, we entered into an Assignment of Claims Agreement (&#8220;Assignment Agreement&#8221;) with Anga M&#8217;Hak Publishing (&#8220;Anga<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">M&#8217;Hak&#8221;) and Edward Raabe, for the consideration of 150,000 shares of common stock and $100,000 in cash out of the proceeds of our proposed private placement.&#160; We believe Anga M&#8217;Hak has a claim to offset the approximately $1,500,000 of the Sacks judgment.&#160; As part of the Assignment Agreement, Anga M&#8217;Hak and Raabe agreed to execute affidavits detailing their entitlement to the above-referenced claims and monies.&#160; In addition, they agreed to appear for depositions and as witnesses in court and to otherwise fully cooperate in our pursuit of these claims.&#160; We believe their affidavits will indicate that Sacks perpetrated fraud by not having the authority to enter into the contract, which wrongfully created the judgment in favor of Sacks.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">31</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Effective March 30, 2010, we entered into a Settlement Agreement and Release (the &#8220;Sacks Settlement&#8221;) with Sacks and with Greg Sacks (&#8220;Greg&#8221;).&#160;&#160;Under the Sacks Settlement, the Company agreed to pay Sacks $100,000 on or before April 15, 2010 and to issue Sacks 1,000,000 shares of its common stock in the form of 10 certificates of 100,000 shares each (the &#8220;Sacks Shares&#8221;).&#160;&#160;The Sacks Settlement called for the Sacks Shares to be delivered to the Company&#8217;s law firm, Solomon Ward Seidenwurm &amp; Smith, LLP (&#8220;SWSS&#8221;) with an irrevocable instruction to mail to Sacks one stock certificate of 100,000 shares per month for 10 consecutive months commencing July 15, 2010. The Sacks Shares will become free-trading upon receipt of a legal opinion from the Company&#8217;s counsel.&#160;&#160;Sacks agreed that it will not directly or indirectly sell, transfer or assign more than 100,000 shares of the Sacks Shares during any thirty day period at any time.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Sacks Settlement provided that, once the Company made the $100,000 payment and delivered the Sacks Shares to SWSS, Sacks would irrevocably waive, release and surrender all rights relating to or arising from its May 28, 2008 judgment against us and would take all actions reasonably requested by us to cause the judgment to be permanently rendered of no force or effect including without limitation by stipulating to set aside and vacate the judgment and cause then entire litigation to be dismissed with prejudice.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">While the Company issued the Sacks Shares to Sacks on March 30, 2010, we were unable to raise the $100,000 payment due April 15, 2010, and therefore were could not perform our obligations under the Sacks Settlement including the delivery of the Sacks Shares to SWSS.&#160;&#160;We are still attempting to raise the $100,000 and, if successful, are hopeful that Sacks will honor the Sacks Settlement, although there is no certainty that Sacks will do so.&#160;&#160;We have recorded the Sacks Shares at their par value of $1,000 and included the amount in prepaid expenses in the Company&#8217;s December 31, 2010 Balance Sheet.&#160;&#160;In the event we are not able to complete the Sacks Settlement, the Sacks Shares will be cancelled.&#160;&#160;If the Sacks Settlement is completed, the Company will value the shares on the date of settlement for use in the calculation of gain or loss on extinguishment of accrued litigation.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt">Fish &amp; Richardson</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On or about May&#160;15, 2008, Fish&#160;&amp; Richardson, P.C. (&#8220;Fish&#8221;) filed an action against us in the Superior Court of California, County of San Diego, asserting claims for breach of a settlement agreement purportedly entered into in connection with fees allegedly owed by us to Fish for Fish&#8217;s providing of legal services on the Company&#8217;s behalf in the approximate amount of $255,000.&#160;&#160;The settlement agreement, dated September 27, 2006, also granted Fish a security interest in all of the Tradenames owned by the Company and all associated goodwill.&#160;&#160;In its response to the Fish action, the Company asserted that the settlement agreement was void and that Fish failed to act as reasonably careful attorneys in connection with their representation of us.&#160;&#160;Fish brought a motion for summary judgment which was heard on April&#160;17, 2009, and the motion was granted.&#160;&#160;On May 21, 2009, a judgment was entered against the Company for $273,835 plus interest of $74,817 through the date of the judgment.&#160;&#160;On September 10, 2009, Fish filed an action to foreclose on their security interest in the Company&#8217;s tradenames, which action was served on the Company&#8217;s registered agent on approximately October 19, 2009.&#160;&#160;Given the Company&#8217;s decision to no longer use its Tradenames, the Company began working on an agreement with Fish to affect an orderly transfer of the Tradenames to Fish and, effective January 19, 2010, we entered into a settlement agreement with Fish wherein the Company agreed to transfer all right, title and interest in its tradenames to Fish and Fish has agreed to acknowledge a full satisfaction of its judgment and to dismiss the Federal Action with prejudice.&#160;&#160;The Fish Settlement Agreement was executed on January 21, 2010 and we recorded a gain on extinguishment of debt of $267,661 in 2010 for this settlement.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">H.G. Fenton</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On or about July 22, 2009, H.G. Fenton Property Company (&#8220;Fenton&#8221;) filed a complaint against the Company in the Superior Court of California, County of San Diego, alleging Breach of Lease at our former office in Carlsbad, <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">California (the &#8220;Carlsbad Lease&#8221;.)&#160;&#160;The complaint claims damages in the amount of $420,000.&#160;&#160;In its answer to the complaint, the Company contends that Fenton has failed to mitigate damages, Fenton&#8217;s damages are speculative, and Fenton made certain representations concerning a lease restructure that the Company relied on to its detriment.&#160;&#160;On March 26, 2010, the Company attended a Case Management Conference during which a tentative trial date was set for January 14, 2011.</font> </font>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><br>
</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Effective December 17, 2010, the Company and Fenton executed a Stipulation for Entry of Judgment and Conditional Dismissal.&#160;&#160;Under the Stipulation, the parties agreed to accept a judgment against the Company in the amount of $294,590.&#160;&#160;The parties also agreed that the Company would pay Fenton a Settlement Payment of $20,000 and 200,000 shares of our common stock, which shares would be free-trading and unrestricted, and Fenton agreed to accept the Settlement Payment in full satisfaction of the judgment amount.&#160;&#160;The Company paid $10,000 of the Settlement Payment in December 2010 through an advance from FITT.&#160;&#160;In January 2011, a shareholder and former officer of the Company transferred 200,000 of his free-trading shares to Fenton on behalf of the Company.&#160;&#160;In March 2011, the Company made the final payment of $10,000 through an advance from FITT in full satisfaction of its obligations under the Stipulation.&#160;&#160;The payments and share issuance were in accordance with the requirements set forth in the settlement.&#160;&#160;In January 2011, the Company issued 200,000 shares to the shareholder and former officer to replace his shares transferred to Fenton.</font></font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>

<div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; WIDTH: 100%">
<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">32</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Christopher Wicks/Defiance</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On May&#160;8, 2007, we were served with a summons and complaint in a lawsuit filed in the San Diego Superior Court by Christopher Wicks and Defiance U.S.A.,&#160;Inc. seeking judgment against us, Edon Moyal and Dan Fleyshman under a contract allegedly calling for the payment of $288,000 in cash plus stock in our subsidiary, Who&#8217;s Your Daddy,&#160;Inc., a California corporation, and a certain percentage of the revenues of that subsidiary.&#160; On February&#160;1, 2008, we entered into a Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release with the plaintiffs pursuant to which we agreed to pay to the plaintiffs the sum of $252,000 under a payment schedule detailed therein.&#160; As security for the settlement payment, defendants Fleyshman and Moyal together pledged 317,210 shares of common stock in the Company owned and held by them.&#160; We are currently in default of the Settlement Agreement.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt">Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April&#160;1, 2005, the Company received a complaint filed by Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy,&#160;Inc., a Florida corporation (&#8220;Daddy&#8221;), alleging that the Company was infringing on Daddy&#8217;s trademark, Who&#8217;s Ya Daddy&#174;, with respect to clothing.&#160;&#160;On April&#160;7, 2006, the Company entered into a settlement agreement with Daddy pursuant to which the Company was granted an exclusive license to use its marks on clothing in exchange for a royalty payment of 6% of gross sales for clothing products in the United States, excluding footwear.&#160;&#160;As part of the settlement, the Company also agreed to remit to Daddy 12% of the licensing revenues received from third parties who the Company granted sublicense to for use of the marks on clothing.&#160;&#160;The Company had not made any of the required payments under the settlement agreement.&#160;&#160;On March&#160;26, 2008, the Company, Dan Fleyshman and Edon Moyal each received a Notice of Levy from the United States District Court for the Southern District of California in the amount of $143,561 allegedly pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement with Daddy.&#160;&#160;The Company settled the debt on March&#160;4, 2009 for $125,000 of which $25,000 was paid through an advance by a shareholder.&#160;&#160;The remaining balance was to be repaid with bi-monthly payments of $10,000 beginning April&#160;30, 2009.&#160;&#160;The Company has not made any additional payments and is in default of the most recent settlement agreement.&#160;&#160;As such, Daddy may elect to declare the recent settlement agreement null and void and resume its pursuit of the amount due under the original settlement agreement, but the Company has not yet been notified that Daddy has chosen to do so.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Straub Distributing&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On July&#160;30, 2008, we entered into a Compromise and Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release (the &#8220;Straub Settlement&#8221;) with Straub Distributing, L.L.C. (&#8220;Straub&#8221;), a California Limited Liability Corporation.&#160; We paid $7,500 on July&#160;30, 2008 and were required to make two additional payments of $7,250 every sixty days for total payments to Straub of $22,000.&#160; As part of the Straub Settlement, the parties executed a Stipulation for Entry of Judgment (the &#8220;Stipulation&#8221;) which could be pursued by Straub in the event any of the payments were late by more than fifteen days.&#160;&#160;The Stipulation was in the amount of $40,000 against us plus attorney fees, costs and expenses to enforce the judgment, and was to be reduced by the amount of payments previously received.&#160; We made one payment of $7,250, but were unable to make additional payments.&#160; On January 2, 2009, Straub filed an action requesting entry of judgment against us in the principal sum of $25,250 as per the Stipulation.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Reuven Rubinson</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On or about September 24, 2009, Leslie T. Gladstone, Trustee for Debtor Reuven Rubinson, filed a complaint against the Company in the United States Bankruptcy Court, Southern District of California, asserting claims for breach of contract, open book account, and turnover of property of the estate.&#160;&#160;The claims stemmed from an employment agreement between Mr. Rubinson and the Company during the time Mr. Rubinson was a former CFO of the Company.&#160;&#160;Damages claimed in the complaint total $130,000 plus interest from July 2, 2007.&#160;&#160;In its response to the complaint, the Company asserted that all debts previously owed to Mr. Rubinson have been paid in prior years either in cash or in shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock and that the Company owes Mr. Rubinson nothing.&#160;&#160;On February 8, 2010 we agreed to a Stipulation to Settle Adversary Proceeding with the Trustee whereby we agreed to forward to the Trustee evidence of the 100,000 shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock which had previously been issued to Mr. Rubinson in complete settlement of the claims.</font></div>

<br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Markstein Beverage</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On December 22, 2009, a Default Judgment was entered against us by Markstein Beverage Co. (&#8220;Markstein&#8221;) in the amount of $5,000.&#160;&#160;The matter, which was heard in Superior Court of California, Small Claims Division, arose from purported unpaid product distribution costs borne by Markstein.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Get Logistics</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On March&#160;19, 2008, a complaint was filed against us by Get Logistics, LLC (formerly known as GE Transport) seeking damages of $30,279 for unpaid shipping charges.&#160; We are attempting to settle this matter.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Worldwide Express</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On March&#160;3, 2009, a judgment was entered against us in favor of Worldwide Express for unpaid delivery services.&#160;&#160;The amount of the judgment was $8,794 inclusive of interest and costs.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">12.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Gain on Extinguishment of Debt and Creditor Obligations</font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Following is a summary of the gain (loss) on extinguishment of debt and creditor obligations for the year ended December 31, 2010.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<td valign="bottom" width="1%" style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 4px double; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>
<td valign="bottom" width="8%" style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 4px double; TEXT-ALIGN: right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">0.0 </font></td>
<td nowrap valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 4px; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">%</font></td>
<td align="right" valign="bottom" width="2%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 4px"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>

</tr></table>
</div>

<td nowrap valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 4px; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Based on federal tax returns filed through December&#160;31, 2010, the Company had available approximately $19,980,000 in U.S. tax net operating loss carryforwards, pursuant to the Tax Reform Act of 1986, which assesses the utilization of a Company&#8217;s net operating&#160;&#160;loss carryforwards resulting from retaining continuity of its business operations and changes within its ownership structure.&#160; Net operating loss carryforwards expire in 20 years for federal income tax reporting purposes.&#160; For Federal income tax purposes, the net operating losses begin to expire in 2026.&#160; State net operating loss carryforwards through December 31, 2010 are approximately $18,782,000 and begin to expire in 2011.&#160; The Company has relied on the issuance of common stock to fund certain operating expenses.&#160; The Company may have experienced a change in ownership as defined in Section&#160;382 of the Internal Revenue Code.&#160; In the event the Company experienced a change in ownership, net operating loss carryforwards for federal income tax reporting will be limited based on the fair value of the Company on the date of change in ownership.&#160; Such change is expected to provide benefit to the Company only upon the attainment of profitability.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During the years ended December&#160;31, 2010 and 2009, the Company&#8217;s valuation allowance increased/(decreased) by approximately $(56,019) and $913,203, respectively.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The United States Federal return years 2007 through 2010 are still subject to tax examination by the United States Internal Revenue Service, however, we do not currently have any ongoing tax examinations.&#160;&#160;The Company is subject to examination by the California Franchise Tax Board for the years 2007 through 2010 and currently does not have any ongoing tax examinations.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">14.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Preferred Stock</font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Company has authorized 20,000,000 shares of preferred stock of which none are issued and outstanding at December&#160;31, 2010 and 2009, respectively.&#160; The outstanding shares at December 31, 2008 were owned by two former officers of the Company, both of whom agreed to cancel their shares in December 2009 for no consideration.&#160; The preferred shares are not convertible into common stock and have no preference rights, however, each share is entitled to four votes on a common stock basis.&#160; The Certificate of Designation related to these shares has not been filed with the State of Nevada and these preferred shares will not have voting power until this filing is accomplished.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">15.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Common Stock</font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Sales of Common Stock</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2010, the Company sold 575,000 shares of our common stock for cash proceeds of $60,000.&#160;&#160;During 2009, we sold 1,800,000 shares of our common stock for cash proceeds of $45,000.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Issuance of Common Stock to Officers and Employees</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2009, the Company issued a total of 17,000,000 shares of common stock to an officer and an employee in connection with Employment Agreements dated August 24, 2009, and recorded stock-based compensation amounting to $425,000 in General and Administrative expense in connection with the issuance.&#160;&#160;A minimum of 15,400,000 of the shares were restricted and subject to lock-up provisions that prohibit the officer and employee from selling, transferring or otherwise encumbering the shares for a period of six (6) months.&#160;&#160;The fair value of the shares was determined based on the closing stock price on the date of issuance and the shares were earned on their respective dates of issuance.</font></div>

<br>
</div>

</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Issuance of Common Stock to Consultants and Advisors</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2010, the Company issued 9,820,000 shares of common stock, valued at $1,068,453 in payment for marketing, representation, production, investor relations, and operating services, including 5,000,000 shares to FITT in connection with our Operating Agreement.&#160;&#160;In addition, we issued the 10,000,000 shares previously committed in 2009.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2009 the Company issued 13,400,000 shares of common stock with an aggregate fair value of $609,200 in payment for marketing, representation, lead generation, and investor relations services as well as for director&#8217;s fees.&#160;&#160;In addition, the Company committed to issue to a consultant&#160;10,000,000 shares with a fair value of $300,000 for marketing, representation and lead generation services.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The fair value of the shares was determined based on the closing stock price on the date of issuance or commitment, and the shares were earned on their respective vesting dates.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During 2010, the Company issued 75,000 shares of common stock a former officer in settlement of accrued salaries and advances owed to him.&#160;&#160;The fair value of the shares of $13,500 was based on the closing stock price on the date of issuance.&#160;&#160;See Note 16.&#160;&#160;In addition, as disclosed in Note 11, effective March 30, 2010 we entered into the Sacks Settlement and issued Sacks 1,000,000 of our common shares.&#160;&#160;Although we have been unable to complete the Sacks Settlement, we have recorded the shares at their par value of $1,000 and included the amount in prepaid expenses in the Company&#8217;s December 31, 2010 Balance Sheet.&#160;&#160;We are still attempting to complete our obligations under the Sacks Settlement and, if successful, are hopeful that Sacks will honor the Sacks Settlement, although there is no certainty that Sacks will do so.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On June&#160;29, 2007, the Company&#8217;s board of directors adopted the 2007 Equity Incentive Plan (the &#8220;2007 Plan&#8221;).&#160; The 2007 Plan provides for the grant of equity awards to directors, officers, other employees, consultants, independent contractors and agents of the Company and its subsidiaries, including stock options to purchase shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock, stock appreciation rights (&#8220;SARs&#8221;), restricted stock, restricted stock units, bonus stock and performance shares.&#160; Up to 833,333 shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock may be issued pursuant to awards granted under the 2007 Plan, subject to adjustment in the event of stock splits and other similar events.&#160; The 2007 Plan is administered by the Board of Directors, and expires ten years after adoption, unless terminated earlier by the Board.&#160; As of December&#160;31, 2010, no stock option grants have been made under the 2007 Plan.&#160;&#160;There was no expense related to stock options during the years ended December 31, 2010 and 2009.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">16.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 3pt">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Agreements</font></div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with MRR Investments</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On January 13, 2010, the Company entered into a Marketing &amp; Representation Agreement with MRR Investments, LLC (the &#8220;MRR Agreement&#8221;), a company with significant experience in marketing and strategic alliances.&#160;&#160;The MRR Agreement called for MRR Investments to provide services in the areas of product endorsement, strategic <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">marketing, product support and strategic introductions.&#160;&#160;Under the MRR Agreement, which has a term of 12 months, MRR received 75,000 shares of our common stock.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on January 13, 2010, the date of issuance, and the Company recorded a stock-based marketing expense of $14,175 during 2010 based on the market price on the date of issuance.</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
<div id="FTR">
<div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">37</font></div>

<div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
<hr style="COLOR: dimgray" noshade size="2">
</div>
</div>

<div id="HDR">
<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Debt Compromise &#8211; Former Officers</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Company entered into letter agreements (the &#8220;Letter Agreements&#8221;) dated January 13, 2010 with two former officers and directors, Dan Fleyshman (&#8220;Fleyshman&#8221;) and Edon Moyal (&#8220;Moyal&#8221;), whereby Fleyshman and Moyal agreed that the Company would not be required to repay indebtedness owed them totaling $630,691 ($200,458 in accrued salaries, $370,026 in unpaid loans and advances, and $60,207 in unpaid services provided).&#160;&#160;Additionally, the Letter Agreements require Fleyshman and Moyal to use their best efforts to liquidate a total of 2,044,428 shares of our common stock (the &#8220;Former Officers&#8217; Shares&#8221;) held in their name, and use the proceeds to repay certain other specified debt (the &#8220;Specified Debt&#8221;), including delinquent payroll taxes.&#160;&#160;There can be no assurance that any of the Former Officers&#8217; Shares can be liquidated or, if liquidated, the proceeds, net of costs of settlement of the Specified Debt (including legal fees) and costs of liquidation, would be sufficient to repay the Specified Debt.&#160;&#160;Therefore, the Company did not provide a release to Fleyshman and Moyal for any liability they may have for the Specified Debt.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The Company entered into a Settlement Agreement and General Release (the &#8220;Officer Settlement Agreement&#8221;) dated January 15, 2010 with another former officer (the &#8220;Officer&#8221;) whereby the Officer agreed to accept 75,000 shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock (the &#8220;Shares&#8221;) as full repayment of indebtedness owed by the Company totaling $183,981 ($178,683 in accrued salaries and $5,298 in unpaid loans and advances).&#160;&#160;The Officer Settlement Agreement provides that the Company will endeavor to obtain an opinion from counsel confirming that the Shares need not contain a restrictive legend under Rule 144.&#160;&#160;The Officer signed the Officer Settlement Agreement on January 19, 2010.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In connection with the Letter Agreements and the Officer Settlement Agreement, the Company recorded an adjustment to Additional Paid-in Capital in the amount of $835,295 during 2010 related to the forgiveness of their debts because of their prior capacity as officers and/or directors.&#160;&#160;The adjustment to Additional Paid-in Capital is comprised of the following:</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Amendments to Sam Maywood Agreement</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On July 28, 2009 the Company entered into a Marketing &amp; Representation Agreement with Sam Maywood M.D. (the &#8220;S. Maywood Agreement&#8221;), a Board Certified Anesthesiologist and pain management specialist, who has extensive experience in understanding the use and benefits of herbal products and their associated marketing.&#160;&#160;On March 3, 2010, the Company entered into Amendment No. 2 to the S. Maywood Agreement which included the issuance of 1,000,000 shares, and on May 1, 2010, the Company entered into Amendment No. 3 to the S. Maywood Agreement which included the issuance of 1,000,000 shares.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on the dates of issuance (March 3, 2010 and May 1, 2010), and the Company recorded stock-based marketing expense of $290,000 during 2010 based on the market prices on the dates of issuance.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with APPL</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On March 23, 2010, the Company entered into a Financial Public Relations Agreement with APPL International Inc. (&#8220;APPL&#8221;) (the &#8220;APPL Agreement&#8221;) under which APPL agreed to provide a variety of public relations services including press release and other correspondence with the public, investors, portfolio managers, brokers and analysts </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">on behalf of the Company.&#160;&#160;The APPL Agreement had a term of six (6) months and could be terminated by either party with immediate notice.&#160;&#160;In connection with the APPL Agreement, the Company agreed to issue 1,000,000 shares of its common stock to an employee of APPL.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on March 23, 2010, the date of issuance, and are being released to APPL&#8217;s employee on an agreed upon schedule.&#160;&#160;The Company recorded stock-based general and administrative expense of $60,000 during 2010 based on the market price on the date of issuance.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with ICA</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April 9, 2010, the Company entered into a Consulting Agreement with Issuers Capital Advisors, LLC (&#8220;ICA&#8221;) (the &#8220;ICA Agreement&#8221;) under which ICA agreed to provide a variety of public relations services including press release and other correspondence with the public, investors, portfolio managers, brokers and analysts on behalf of the Company.&#160;&#160;The ICA Agreement had a term of six (6) months and could be terminated by either party after 60 days for any reason.&#160;&#160;In connection with the ICA Agreement, the Company agreed to issue 570,000 shares of its common stock to ICA.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on April 9, 2010, the date of issuance, and are being released to ICA on an agreed upon schedule.&#160;&#160;The Company recorded stock-based general and administrative expense of $57,000 during 2010 based on the market price on the date of issuance.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">&#160;</div>

<div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160; </font></div>

</div>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with Sports 1 Marketing LLC</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In 2009, we entered into a Marketing &amp; Lead Generation Agreement with LSSE, LLC (&#8220;LSSE&#8221;) (the &#8220;LSSE Agreement&#8221;).&#160;&#160;Under the LSSE Agreement, LSSE agreed to provide a variety of services including marketing, public relations, and merchandising services, including endorsement of our products by NFL Hall of Fame quarterback, Warren Moon, who was affiliated with a principal of LSSE.&#160;&#160;The Company agreed to compensate LSSE by issuing them 10,000,000 shares of our common stock, 1,000,000 of which were to be issued to Mr. Moon.&#160;&#160;LSSE could not fulfill their obligations under the LSSE Agreement and they instructed us to inform our transfer agent not to issue the 10,000,000 shares.&#160;&#160;Due to recent changes at LSSE, Mr. Moon agreed to perform the obligations under the LSSE Agreement, under the same terms and conditions, through a company he owned named Sports 1 Marketing LLC (&#8220;Sports 1&#8221;), for the same 10,000,000 shares committed to LSSE.&#160;&#160;Mr. Moon and Sports 1 have the same athlete and media contacts to be able to perform the consulting services outlined in the LSSE Agreement.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April 21, 2010, we issued the 10,000,000 previously committed shares, with 1,000,000 shares issued and released to Mr. Moon immediately.&#160;&#160;The remaining 9,000,000 shares are being released to Sports 1 on an agreed upon schedule.&#160;&#160;All shares were previously expensed in 2009 upon the initial agreement with LSSE and no additional expense was recognized in 2010.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">FITT Operating Agreement</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As discussed in Note 1, the Company entered into an Operating Agreement with FITT on August 12, 2010.&#160;&#160;Under the terms of&#160;the Operating Agreement, FITT will perform certain operating services for the Company, including among other things, selling, marketing, producing and distributing of the FITT Energy Shot, and will pay all costs and expenses involved with performing these services.&#160;&#160;For its part, FITT will pay the Company a royalty of $0.05 for each bottle sold of the FITT Energy Shot.&#160;&#160;This royalty obligation will first be offset against amounts owed by us to FITT, and next by the IRS Levy discussed in Note 6, after which royalty payments will be made directly to the Company.&#160;&#160;FITT will also have the option, but not be required, to advance funds to the Company if necessary.&#160;&#160;The Company agreed to issue FITT 5,000,000 of its shares of Common Stock in connection with the Operating Agreement, which shares were fully vested, and therefore valued for accounting purposes, as of the date of the agreement.&#160;&#160;While FITT has indicated it will use the shares as an inducement to attract investment dollars from interested investors, it is not required to do so.&#160;&#160;FITT will incur and record all sales, costs and operating expenses connected with its performance of services in connection with the Operating Agreement, and all cash, inventory and other assets resulting from either the invested dollars or from FITT&#8217;s operations will be the property of FITT.&#160;&#160;While FITT will enjoy the benefit of any profits earned through its performance of the operating services, it will also bear the responsibility of any losses and of raising capital.&#160;&#160;The Company recorded stock-based selling and marketing expense of $500,000, based on the market price on the date of the agreement, during 2010.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">From the inception of the Operating Agreement through December 31, 2010, the amount of the royalty obligation owed to the Company by FITT was insignificant.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with Tribe Communications</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center">
<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On October 15, 2010, the Company entered into a Marketing and Production Agreement with Tribe Communications, Inc. (&#8220;Tribe&#8221;) (the &#8220;Tribe Agreement&#8221;).&#160;&#160;Under the Tribe Agreement, which has a term of three months, Tribe has agreed to conduct and broadcast three (3) radio and four (4) television interviews with our CEO on the Big Biz Show, a business radio and internet television talk show broadcast out of San Diego, California.&#160;&#160;The interviews with our CEO, which are intended to create public awareness of the Company, discussed the rollout of the FITT Energy Shot and the impact that may have for the Company as a result of the royalty arrangement with FITT.&#160;&#160;The interviews, each of which will be approximately 25 minutes in length, will be re-broadcast 21 times (both radio and television) during the term of the Tribe Agreement.&#160;&#160;In addition, Tribe has agreed to provide a 5 to 10 minute live, on-air radio review of any newsworthy Company event within 24 hours of such event taking place.&#160;&#160;In connection with the Tribe Agreement, the Company agreed to issue to Tribe 900,000 shares of its common stock, 700,000 of which would come directly from the Company and carry a Rule 144 restriction and 200,000 of which would be free-trading and transferred to Tribe on behalf of the Company by a shareholder and former officer of the Company.&#160;&#160;The shares issued were fully vested as of the dates of issuance and transfer, and the Company recorded a selling and marketing expense of $117,000 in 2010, which equates to the aggregate value of all shares issued to Tribe based on the market value on the date of the Tribe Agreement.&#160;&#160;On November 22, 2010, the Company issued 200,000 shares to the shareholder and former officer to replace the shares he transferred to Tribe on behalf of the Company.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On November 17, 2010, the Company reached an agreement with Pentony Enterprises LLC (&#8220;Pentony&#8221;) (the &#8220;Pentony Agreement&#8221;) under which Pentony agreed to provide investor relations services to the Company, including among other things coverage on Pentony&#8217;s website, StockGuru.com, blog coverage on the website, trade alerts, and audio interviews.&#160;&#160;The Pentony Agreement expired February 15, 2011.&#160;&#160;In connection with the Pentony Agreement, the Company agreed to issue 275,000 shares of its common stock to Pentony.&#160;&#160;The shares issued by the Company were fully vested on November 17, 2010, the date of issuance, and the Company recorded a general and administrative expense of $30,278 in 2010 in connection with the agreement.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with E 2 Investments, LLC</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On January 24, 2011, the Company entered into a Consulting Agreement with E 2 Investments, LLC (the &#8220;E 2 Agreement&#8221;), a company with significant experience in acquisitions, joint ventures, business relations and public company relations.&#160;&#160;The E 2 Agreement calls for E 2 Investments to provide advice on financing, acquisitions, joint ventures, public company relations and to introduce the Company to potential investors.&#160;&#160;In connection with the E 2 Agreement, the Company agreed to issue to E 2 Investments 700,000 shares of its common stock, 300,000 of which would come directly from the Company and carry a Rule 144 restriction and 400,000 of which would be free-trading and transferred to E 2 Investments on behalf of the Company by a shareholder and employee of the Company.&#160;&#160;The shares issued were fully vested as of the dates of issuance and transfer, and the Company will record a general and administrative expense of $31,500 in 2011, which equates to the aggregate value of all shares issued to E 2 Investments based on the market value on the date of the E 2 Agreement.&#160;&#160;On January 31, 2011, the Company issued 400,000 shares to the shareholder and employee to replace the shares he transferred to E 2 Investments on behalf of the Company.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with Gene Stohler</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On February 23, 2011, the Company entered into a Consulting Agreement with Gene Stohler (the &#8220;Stohler Agreement&#8221;), an individual with significant experience in marketing and strategic alliances.&#160;&#160;The Stohler Agreement calls for Mr. Stohler to provide services in the areas of product development and endorsement, strategic marketing, product support and introductions to potential strategic partners for the Company.&#160;&#160;Under the Stohler Agreement, which has a term of 12 months, Mr. Stohler received 100,000 shares of our common stock.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on February 23, 2011, the date of issuance, and the Company will record a stock-based marketing expense of $7,000 during 2011 based on the market price on the date of issuance.</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><br>
</div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with The Street Awareness</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On March 22, 2011, we entered into an agreement with The Street Awareness (&#8220;Street Awareness&#8221;).&#160;&#160;Under the agreement, Street Awareness has agreed to conduct a comprehensive investor relations campaign of approximately 75 days to improve market awareness of the Company and our business prospects.&#160;&#160;As consideration for the services <font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">to be performed by Street Awareness, we have agreed to pay a fee of $250,000, payable $50,000 per week for 5 weeks beginning April 11, 2011.&#160;&#160;The Company currently does not have the necessary funds to make any payment under this agreement, but is attempting to negotiate funding with FITT or with a shareholder of the Company.&#160;&#160;There can be no assurance that we will be successful in raising the required funding to pay for this agreement and, as such, the ability to execute this investor relations campaign is uncertain.</font> </font>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><br>
</font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Agreement with S.A Frederick &amp; Co., LLC</font></font></div>

<div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: 1.25; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On March 23, 2011, the Company entered into a Consulting Agreement with S.A. Frederick &amp; Co., LLC (&#8220;Consultant&#8221;) (the &#8220;Frederick Agreement&#8221;), an entity with significant experience in marketing and strategic alliances.&#160;&#160;The Frederick Agreement calls for the Consultant to provide services in the areas of product development and endorsement, strategic marketing, product support and introductions to potential strategic partners for the Company.&#160;&#160;Under the Frederick Agreement, which has a term of four (4) months, the Company agreed to pay four (4) monthly payments of $7,500 each and issue Consultant 600,000 shares of our common stock to be released to Consultant on an agreed upon schedule.&#160;&#160;The shares were fully vested on March 23, 2011, the date of issuance, and the Company will record a marketing expense of $78,000 during 2011 representing the amount of cash plus the value of the shares on the date of issuance.</font></font></div>
